meta.title: Sunder Implementation Phasing Plan v2 — Scope Cut
meta.source: roadmap docs/Sunder - Source of Truth/product-dev/01-App Spec.md §17
meta.scopeDecision: Simplified to: AI CRM + autopilot + connections + email + Telegram + agent-generated views. Cut skills system, sandbox, scraping lanes, scaling infra, voice, eval, model routing, structured AI input. Restored CRM configurability (PR 15a). Agent-generated views (PR 42a) moved to Phase 3 using catalog-based JSON spec approach (json-render pattern) — no sandbox needed. Sandboxed code-gen views (Tasklet instant-app pattern) deferred to PR 42b. Added Stripe billing as a Phase 4 scope exception using hosted Checkout + Customer Portal + webhook sync onto the clients table. Added PR 38c for app-enforced monthly message caps keyed off the mirrored client plan.
changelog: 2026-03-05: v2 created — scope cut from 48 PRs to 30 (13 done + 17 to build). Reordered into new phases. Original plan preserved at 2026-03-01-implementation-phasing-plan.json.
2026-03-05: Removed PR 19a (Agent tasks). (Superseded by entry 6 — reverted to dedicated agent_todo table.)
2026-03-05: Restored PR 22 (Context recovery + thread compaction) into Phase 2. Required for autopilot thread which accumulates messages every 6h.
2026-03-05: Explicitly cut goals table (goals written to MEMORY.md). Confirmed per-thread approval discovery (no Queue tab). Autopilot priority order rewritten for simplified crm_tasks model.

2026-03-05: Tasklet gap analysis fixes — PR 15: user email in system-reminder, connection state slot, state/ directory convention in platform instructions. PR 20: webhook + RSS trigger types. PR 26: wire connection state into system-reminder.

2026-03-05: Split agent todo from CRM tasks. Reverted unified task model (source+payload on crm_tasks). Added dedicated agent_todo table + manage_todo/list_todo tools in PR 15 (Tasklet-parity, minimal schema: title+payload, exists-or-deleted). CRM task tools unchanged. Updated autopilot pulse prompt (PR 19-5) to use list_todo() for agent work + search_tasks() for CRM work.

2026-03-05: CRM tool review vs Attio MCP + HubSpot MCP. Added PR 15a (CRM configurability — dynamic vocabulary + custom fields) in Phase 2 after PR 15. Restored from cut PR 38a with expanded scope: Layer 1 (vocabulary from crm_config) + Layer 2 (custom fields via JSONB). Added PR 42a (agent-generated views) as exploratory Phase 5 placeholder inspired by Tasklet instant apps. Removed PR 38a from cutPRs list.

2026-03-06: Expanded PR 38 onboarding scope to capture the auth funnel shipped ahead of full onboarding: dedicated /login + /register pages, Supabase Google OAuth callback flow, unified /chat post-auth redirect, display_name fallback fix for OAuth users, and the remaining hosted Supabase/Google setup checklist needed before launch.

2026-03-07: Generative UI approach decision. PR 42a rewritten: catalog-based JSON view specs (inspired by Vercel Labs json-render) replaces sandboxed iframe approach. Agent outputs JSON conforming to a Zod component catalog, frontend renders deterministically — no sandbox, no CSP, no bridge API. Moved PR 42a from Phase 5 to Phase 3 (no longer blocked by EXEC-04 sandbox). Added PR 42b as deferred Phase 5 escape hatch for full Tasklet-style sandboxed code gen if catalog proves insufficient. Added architecture decision UX-10.

2026-03-07: Added PR 22a (multimodal chat — image attachments) and PR 22b (tool output rendering — json-render + tool approval UI) to Phase 2. Both follow vercel/chatbot patterns with minimal drift. PR 22a: file upload via Supabase Storage, PreviewAttachment component, parts-based messaging, stop button. PR 22b: json-render for tool output display, addToolApprovalResponse wiring with approve/deny buttons. Reference docs in roadmap docs/Sunder - Source of Truth/references/vercel-chatbot/.

2026-03-07: Added PR 20a (Suggested automations — template prompt cards) to Phase 2 after PR 20. KISS approach following Tasklet pattern: a template is just a pre-filled chat message. Hardcoded TS array of { id, title, description, category, icon, prompt }. Cards shown on /automations page + suggestion chips in chat empty state. Click → navigate to /chat with prompt pre-filled → agent handles setup conversationally. No template engine, no activation flow, no special UI. Reference: roadmap docs/Sunder - Source of Truth/references/template-automations.md.

2026-03-09: Expanded PR 38 onboarding with conversational bootstrap pattern inspired by OpenClaw's BOOTSTRAP.md. Replaced single PR38-8 task with 6 tasks (PR38-8 through PR38-13). Core principle: 'Don't interrogate. Don't be robotic. Just talk.' The first conversation IS the product. Full comparison doc: docs/product/references/2026-03-09-openclaw-onboarding-comparison.md.

2026-03-09: Resolved 4 onboarding design decisions after OpenClaw verbatim comparison. (1) Explicit preferences, not inferred — follow OpenClaw: agent explicitly asks about boundaries, communication style, preferences collaboratively. (2) Pre-structured USER.md + SOUL.md templates with clear fields — follow OpenClaw. (3) Explicit completion moment — follow OpenClaw: agent acknowledges transition warmly, not a silent DB flag. (4) Channel setup for existing users via natural conversation, not re-onboarding — follow OpenClaw: onboarding is a one-time ritual. Agent name 'Sunder' is fixed; everything else co-created. System prompt injection (KISS) instead of BOOTSTRAP.md file in storage.

2026-03-07: Out-of-plan extension of PR 22 (done): PR 22c — Block Storage + Context Management Alignment. Adds block index utilities for compaction summaries (DATA-10), stores ALL tool call args+results to storage, embeds mechanical block index in compaction summaries for post-compaction recovery (SESSION-07), adds <context-management> and <autonomous-mode> instructions. Tasklist: docs/product/tasks/2026-03-07-pr22c-block-storage-context-management-tasklist.md.

2026-03-09: PR 22c scope rewrite for zero Tasklet drift. Removed block index (YAGNI — agent recovers via read_file, not index lookup). Confirmed persistence-time truncation is correct (Tasklet does the same — fixed size threshold, permanent marker, not budget-based). Scope is now 6 tasks: (1) store ALL tool call args+results to block storage regardless of size, (2) trigger event pruning during compaction (mechanical title+source extraction, separate from LLM summarization), (3) structured compaction summary format (## User Instructions / ## Workflow / ## Resources / ## Current Focus), (4) <context-management> instructions in platform-instructions.ts, (5) remove appendArtifactRecoveryNote hack, (6) threshold review (5KB vs Tasklet's ~100KB). Formalized as PR entry in Phase 2 after PR 22b. Tasklist rewritten: docs/product/tasks/2026-03-09-pr22c-context-management-alignment-tasklist.md.

2026-03-09: Added PR 22d (read_file image support + negative line indices) to Phase 2 as an explicit scope exception after review. Scope is intentionally narrow: image files only, explicit AI SDK toModelOutput JSON/image-data handling, negative line indices, preserve existing text/directory read_file contract, PDFs deferred.

2026-03-10: Expanded PR 22d scope to include PDF support. AI SDK v6 file-data content part lets models read raw PDF bytes natively (Anthropic + Gemini). No rendering library needed. Added 10 MB file size guard. toModelOutput now returns image-data for images, file-data for PDFs, json for text/directory. Title updated to 'read_file multimodal (images + PDFs) + negative line indices'.

2026-03-09: Added PR 22e (Absolute agent paths — /agent/ prefix). Model-boundary-only refactor: all model-facing paths become absolute /agent/ paths. Internal storage, DB, frontend, API routes unchanged. Thin toStoragePath/toModelPath conversion at tool boundary (~9 files). Validated with Tasklet developer. Design doc: docs/designs/absolute-agent-paths.md.

2026-03-10: Added PR 26a (System skill files — bundled fallback). Verbatim Tasklet creating-connections skill + direct-api-connection guide, stored as static .md files in codebase, served via read_file fallback for skills/system/ paths. No per-client seeding, no skill_registry. System prompt + tool descriptions already reference these paths (wired in PRs 25-26). SKILL-05 pattern adapted for system skills: bundled in code instead of per-client storage.

2026-03-10: PR 33 approval scope clarified after implementation review. Mechanical gate now targets destructive actions plus connection tool activation: CRM deletes, delete_connection, manage_activated_tools_for_connections, and the delete action inside manage_active_triggers. Non-destructive CRM writes, links, and unlinks stay auto-run. Future outbound send tools adopt the same gate when those tool surfaces ship.

2026-03-10 (reorg): Added 5 previously untracked Phase 1 entries: p1-crm-tool-consolidation, p1-crm-ux, p1-chat-sdk-convergence, p1-chatbot-zero-drift (all done), p1-whatsapp-spike (cut). Added note to PR 26 about sub-PRs 26b-26e.

2026-03-10: Added PR 8a (Google Maps drive-time tool) as a narrow Phase 1 scope exception. Rationale: Chief of Staff transit automations need real drive times, direct_api is still a v1 stub, and a small built-in read-only tool is the minimal path.

2026-03-11: Pulled FOUND-07 forward into Phase 4 as PR 38b (Stripe billing + settings surface). Implementation uses hosted Stripe Checkout, Stripe Customer Portal, and webhook-driven sync onto the clients table, following current Stripe webhook guidance instead of the earlier two-event draft.
2026-03-11: (Removed) PR 8b Excalidraw diagram tool — cut from scope.

2026-03-11: Added PR 38c (Message usage caps + quota UX) to Phase 4. Quotas are app-enforced monthly message counters in Supabase, keyed off clients.plan_name rather than Stripe metered billing. Count only brand-new user-authored inbound chat turns. Free = 100/month, Pro = 500/month, Max = 2,000/month. UI surfaces live in /pricing, /settings, and chat; get_user_quota_status remains omitted as an agent-facing tool.

2026-03-11: Clarified PR 42a scope to include compact snapshot charts (bar, donut, funnel) inside show_view, using narrow custom chart components rendered through json-render. This intentionally stops short of live analytics/reporting: no query-backed refresh, no filters, no pinning, no workspace report surface.

2026-03-11: Added PR 46 (CRM working surfaces) to Phase 4 as an explicit out-of-plan polish item. Scope: unify Deals into one table/board workspace, add a Tasks calendar, add narrow quick edit on high-value CRM surfaces, preserve full detail pages, and keep saved views/reporting out of scope. Naming intentionally avoids collision with PR 42a agent-generated views.

2026-03-12: Added PR 8b (math.js calculate tool) to Phase 3. Small built-in tool (sibling to PR 8a drive-time). General-purpose expression evaluator using math.js — agent writes math expressions, mathjs evaluates them safely. Covers commission calculations, amortization, unit conversions, and any ad-hoc arithmetic. No sandbox needed. Hardened math.js instance disables dangerous functions (import, evaluate, parse, simplify, derivative, resolve, createUnit, reviver). Old PR 8b (Excalidraw) cut from scope.

2026-03-13: Added PR 42a-i (json-render inline mode migration) to Phase 3 after PR 42a. Migrates agent-generated views from show_view tool-call pattern to json-render native inline mode. LLM emits spec JSONL inside ```spec fences; pipeJsonRender() transforms stream; useJsonRenderMessage() + segment-based rendering on client. Deletes show_view tool, ViewCard, ShowViewInline. catalog.prompt({ mode: 'inline' }) replaces hand-rolled getViewCatalogPrompt(). Handover: docs/product/handovers/json-render-inline-mode-handover.md.

2026-03-13: Added PR 42a-p (view component visual polish) to Phase 4. Design polish pass for all 7 custom CRM view components (StatMetric, DealCard, ContactCard, TaskItem, BarChartPanel, DonutChartPanel, FunnelChartPanel). Tighter spacing, better card proportions, improved chart colors, responsive refinements.

2026-03-13: Added 6 production hardening PRs to Phase 4 (PR 47a-f). Pre-launch reliability and security: error boundaries, env validation, health endpoint, security headers, Sentry integration, API rate limiting. Identified during pre-prod QA audit. Can be assigned to another dev.

prs.title: CRM configurability — dynamic vocabulary + custom fields
prs.note: Restored from cut PR 38a with expanded scope. Informed by Attio MCP (schema introspection, upsert) and HubSpot MCP (activity feed) comparison. Layer 1: vocabulary swaps via crm_config (deal stages, contact types, entity labels). Layer 2: custom fields via JSONB columns. Agent configures CRM via chat; tool schemas and UI adapt at runtime. Real-estate defaults as fallback for zero-config users. PR15c ships custom fields in record drawers only; dynamic table columns are deferred to the first task of PR15d or a standalone micro-PR.

tasks.task: Migration: add custom_fields JSONB DEFAULT '{}' to contacts, deals, crm_tasks tables

Define crm_config Zod schema: deal_label, deal_stages, contact_types, interaction_types, deal_contact_roles, deal_custom_fields, contact_custom_fields (each field: key, label, type text|number|currency|date|select, options?, required?)
load_crm_config helper: reads crm_config for clientId, returns typed config with real-estate defaults as fallback. Called once at runner start, passed into tool factories
Dynamic Zod schemas: tool parameter schemas for stage, contact type, interaction type, deal-contact role pull from config instead of hardcoded enums
Dynamic tool descriptions: .describe() strings template from config — 'Create a new {deal_label}' with valid enum values listed

Custom fields in create/update tools: create_contact, update_contact, create_deal, update_deal, create_task, update_task accept optional custom_fields object, validated against config schema at runtime
Custom fields in search results: search_contacts, search_deals, search_tasks return custom_fields in response
configure_crm tool: agent writes to crm_config table. Accepts partial updates. Validates against PR15a-2 schema. Returns updated config
Platform instructions template CRM vocab: inject current vocabulary into platform instructions layer — entity labels, stages, custom field definitions — so LLM knows valid values without tool calls

CRM pages: dynamic labels + columns — Contact/Deal/Task pages read crm_config via API route. PR15c ships custom fields in drawers only; dynamic custom-field table columns move to PR15d or a standalone micro-PR. Stage filter uses config values. Entity labels pulled from config
Fallback defaults: if crm_config is empty/missing, fall back to current real-estate vocabulary. Zero-config for real estate users

User says 'I need to track policy number and coverage amount on deals' → agent adds custom fields → create_deal accepts them → deal/contact/task drawers show the new fields; table columns are deferred

prs.title: Company object — complete the CRM triad
prs.note: Adds Company as the third standard CRM object, completing the universal Company-Contact-Deal triad (matches Twenty, Attio, HubSpot, Salesforce). Autopilot system prompt with simplified priority order adapted from §7.2 (TRIG-08). Two surfaces: agent_todo (list_todo for interrupted agent work with resume payload) and crm_tasks (search_tasks for CRM follow-ups). Priority: 1-resume interrupted work (list_todo, read payload for next step) → 2-check overdue CRM tasks (search_tasks where due_date < now) → 3-act on monitored triggers (deal changes, inbound) → 4-follow up with user on unanswered questions → 5-handle stale CRM tasks (open too long) → 6-research/prepare for upcoming tasks → 7-get to know user (sparse USER.md) → 8-engage user (nudge, pending approvals) → 9-propose new CRM tasks (create_task) or self-todos (manage_todo add) → 10-create momentum (break large work into smaller pieces)

prs.title: Suggested automations — template prompt cards
prs.note: Solves the blank-canvas problem. Follows Tasklet pattern exactly: a template is just a pre-filled chat message. User clicks a card → navigates to /chat with the prompt text pre-filled → sends it → agent handles everything conversationally (asks clarifying questions, writes subagent .md to storage, creates trigger via setup_trigger). No template engine, no activation flow, no special UI beyond the cards themselves. Templates are a hardcoded TS array — each is just { id, title, description, category, icon, prompt }. Chat empty state also shows a few suggestion chips pulling from the same catalog.

prs.title: Block storage + context management alignment (zero Tasklet drift)
prs.note: Extends PR 22 (done). Aligns Sunder's context management with Tasklet's proven production patterns for permanent sessions. Two separate layers: (1) size-based truncation at persistence time with block storage for all tool calls, (2) trigger event pruning + structured summary at compaction time. Confirmed via empirical Tasklet testing: truncation is persistence-time, fixed threshold, permanent. Recovery always through block storage. No block index needed — agent uses read_file with deterministic paths.

prs.title: Agent-generated views (inline, json-render)
prs.note: Agent can respond with visual components (stat cards, tables, kanban boards, compact snapshot charts) rendered inline in chat using Vercel Labs json-render. Uses a narrow catalog of documented @json-render/shadcn components plus 7 custom CRM/view components (StatMetric, DealCard, ContactCard, TaskItem, BarChartPanel, DonutChartPanel, FunnelChartPanel). Tool-based approach: agent calls show_view tool which outputs a JSON spec + pre-computed state. Frontend detects the tool output in the chat UI and renders via json-render's Renderer + StateProvider. No saved/pinned views, no DB migration, no sidebar changes, and no live analytics/reporting layer. Views live in chat only — user asks again for fresh data. Design doc: docs/product/designs/pr31-agent-generated-views.md

prs.title: Onboarding flow
prs.note: v2 simplification: bootstrap files + first chat only. Skip guided interview and deep profile phase. Auth funnel work can land ahead of the rest of onboarding: dedicated login/signup pages, Google-first Supabase auth, unified post-auth redirect to /chat, and a display_name fallback fix so OAuth users bootstrap cleanly into clients. Conversational onboarding inspired by OpenClaw's BOOTSTRAP.md pattern: agent's first message is warm and personal ('Hey, I just came online — who am I working with?'), co-discovers user context through natural chat rather than a form wizard, and writes findings to USER.md + SOUL.md in real time. No interrogation, no robotic checklist — just talk. The first conversation itself is the product.

tasks.task: Quick activation auth surface: dedicated /register and /login pages with a high-conversion split layout, Google as primary CTA, and email/password fallback
Supabase Google OAuth flow: signInWithOAuth on the client, /auth/callback code exchange, sanitized next redirect, and unified post-auth landing on /chat
Redirect consistency across auth surfaces: landing CTA points to signup, email confirmation lands on /chat, and middleware/auth pages agree on chat as the default home
Migration: setup_progress table
Update clients bootstrap/display_name fallback for social sign-in metadata: prefer display_name → full_name → name → email so Google users do not default to raw email when profile data is available

Document prelaunch hosted auth checklist: apply the display_name migration to the hosted Supabase project, add Auth URL allow-list entries for localhost, Vercel previews, and the final production domain, create the Google OAuth web client, and enable the Google provider in Supabase Auth

Document the access needed to finish hosted auth setup: Supabase migration apply rights, Supabase Auth URL Configuration access, Supabase Auth Providers access, and either the Google OAuth client ID/secret or equivalent Google Cloud project access

Bootstrap system prompt injection: conditional block appended to system prompt on first chat (when setup_progress incomplete). Contains conversational first-run instructions inspired by OpenClaw BOOTSTRAP.md. Agent's opening message is warm and personal. No interrogation, no form wizard — just talk. KISS: no extra file in storage, no deletion ritual. Injection removed after completion. Fallback heuristic: if USER.md has been modified from template, treat as onboarded even if setup_progress wasn't marked.

First-chat co-discovery flow (OpenClaw-style explicit, not inferred): agent explicitly asks about user's name, agency/brokerage, specializations (HDB/condo/landed/commercial), years of experience, market areas, and communication style preferences. Also explicitly asks about boundaries and preferences ('What matters to you? How do you want me to communicate? Anything I should never do?'). Agent writes findings to USER.md in real time via write_file. Prerequisite: USER.md template must have pre-structured fields (name, preferred name, agency, specializations, market areas, experience, communication style, context section).

SOUL.md co-creation (OpenClaw-style collaborative, not auto-calibrated): agent and user discuss together how the agent should behave — tone, proactivity, briefing detail, boundaries. Agent name 'Sunder' is fixed; everything else is co-created. Agent writes to SOUL.md via write_file. Prerequisite: SOUL.md template must have structured sections (voice, working style, boundaries, communication preferences) with enough body for co-creation to feel real. Default SOUL.md is a sensible starting point, first chat refines it collaboratively.

Bootstrap completion with closing moment (OpenClaw-style ritual): after first conversation, agent acknowledges the transition with a warm closing ('I've got everything I need. From here on, I'll just be myself. Let's get to work.'). System marks onboarding complete in setup_progress. Subsequent sessions skip bootstrap injection and load normal SOUL.md + USER.md context. One-time ritual, never re-triggers. When Telegram ships (Phase 5), existing users get a mention in natural conversation — not a re-onboarding.

First useful output within the onboarding chat: agent demonstrates value immediately — e.g. creates a CRM contact from something the user mentioned, drafts a brief market note, or sets up a task. The first conversation is productive, not just setup.

Chat welcome state for new users: when a brand-new user lands on /chat with no threads, show a warm welcome card with the agent's first message pre-loaded (not a blank chat). Returning users see the normal chat empty state with template cards.

testCriteria: New user can sign up with Google or email, lands in /chat, and sees the dedicated auth funnel instead of placeholder auth pages
OAuth users create clients rows with a human-friendly display_name derived from provider metadata when available
Hosted launch checklist is explicit: redirect URLs, Google provider credentials, migration apply, and required Supabase permissions are documented before domain cutover
New user lands on /chat, agent greets them warmly and starts a natural conversation — no forms, no wizards
By end of first conversation, USER.md and SOUL.md contain real user context learned through chat
Agent demonstrates value during onboarding chat (creates a contact, sets a task, or drafts something useful)
Second session loads normally with personalized SOUL.md — bootstrap does not re-trigger
New user goes through onboarding, first useful output in <10 min

prs.title: Message usage caps + quota UX
prs.note: Adds app-enforced monthly message caps on top of PR 38b's mirrored plan state.
